note: Grid dimensions read identically and repeated on S-02 (spread footing plan), S-03 (pile footing plan), S-04 (ground floor beam plan), and S-05 (tie-beam plan) -- all 4 sheets print the same '9.60' (3x3.20) x-axis total and '14.55' (4.00+2.00+3.60+1.80+3.15) y-axis total, cross-confirming the grid with no discrepancy. No dummy/prime grid lines were found in any structural sheet read so far (S-01 through S-14) -- the building footprint is simply irregular (rows F/B/A only have columns 2-3 or partial columns, not a full 1-4 span), which does not require a dummy grid, unlike house #1 which had genuine overhang dimensions beyond the named grid. If the architecture-discipline extraction (pages 03-21, being read separately) finds a dimension beyond grid 4/A or before grid 1/F requiring a dummy line, that must be reported back and added here rather than invented independently by whichever pass finds it.

building_footprint_note: Irregular footprint, not a full rectangle: rows F, B, A only have columns 2-3 (or a subset) present -- e.g. footing plan S-02 has no footing at F-2... actually F row has all 4 (F-1,F-2,F-3,F-4), but B and A rows only have columns 2 and 3 (B-2,B-3,A-2,A-3), consistent with a porch/carport extension south of grid C occupying only the middle bay.
warnings: No dummy grid lines found yet -- architecture-discipline pages (03-21) not yet read at time of this file's creation; if an overhang/extension dimension beyond the named grid turns up there, come back and add a dummy grid entry here.
